def: Change for the worse; deterioration; morbid perversion.
The beam which supports the spindle socket of the runner in agrinding mill. Knight.
One who writes epistles; a correspondent. Mary Cowden Clarke.
A person who is not of full age, or who has not attained theage of legal capacity; a person under the age of twenty-one years; aminor.
Long and tedious talk without much substance; superfluity ofwords.
The betel nut. Balfour (Cyc. of India).
Natural gift or talent; ability; wit; ingenuity. [Obs.][Written also ingenie.] Becon.
Apt to let things fall, or to let them slip away; slippery;careless.
Measuring thirty inches by twenty-five; -- said ofportraitures. Three-quarter length, a portrait showing the figure tothe hips only.
Rendered fierce or frantic. [Obs.] "Like one enfeloned ordistraught." Spenser.
